title: Learn programming from zero to real-world projects
subtitle: Code Explorer is a modern programming learning platform for students, beginners, working professionals, and career switchers. Start with logical thinking, learn through practice, and build real projects.

audienceSection.title: Not just for students, this platform supports many learning goals
audienceSection.desc: Each learner group needs a different approach: students need visual learning, beginners need simple explanations, working adults need practical applications, and career switchers need a clear roadmap.

students.title: Students (Grades 6-12)
students.desc: Learn logical thinking, programming basics, and build small projects.

beginners.title: Absolute Beginners
beginners.desc: Start from zero with step-by-step guidance and no prior experience required.

workingAdults.title: Working Professionals
workingAdults.desc: Learn Python, automation, Excel processing, data analysis, and practical tools.

careerSwitchers.title: Career Switchers
careerSwitchers.desc: Build a solid foundation before moving into Web Development, AI, Data, or Automation.

pathSection.title: From Computational Thinking to Python, Web Development, AI, and Automation
pathSection.desc: The platform is designed to support multiple programming languages and courses. In the future, you can add JavaScript, C++, Java, SQL, AI, or Data courses without changing the overall architecture.

features: Quizzes and exercises after every lesson

learningPaths.title: Python Foundation
learningPaths.desc: Variables, conditions, loops, functions, lists, files, and beginner projects.

learningPaths.title: JavaScript Web
learningPaths.desc: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, and modern web application development.

learningPaths.title: Data & Automation
learningPaths.desc: Excel processing, file handling, data analysis, and office automation.

learningPaths.title: AI Beginner
learningPaths.desc: Python fundamentals, data handling, machine learning basics, and AI applications.

methodSection.title: Programming does not start with memorizing syntax
methodSection.desc: Learners are guided through understanding problems, breaking them into smaller tasks, writing solutions, testing results, and improving their projects.

steps.title: Placement Assessment
steps.desc: Take a thinking test to evaluate logic skills and identify the most suitable learning path.

steps.title: Choose a Learning Path
steps.desc: Select Python, Web Development, AI, Automation, or another programming track.

steps.title: Learn and Practice
steps.desc: Study lessons, complete quizzes, write code, and solve practical exercises.

steps.title: Build Projects
steps.desc: Create real projects to reinforce knowledge and build your personal portfolio.

cta.title: Not sure where to begin? Take the thinking test first
cta.desc: The placement test helps determine whether a learner should start with logical thinking fundamentals, Python Foundation, or an accelerated learning path.
